_note: Recap library for delivered ZABAL Gamez workshops. Newest first. recording/youtube/transcript/thumbnail optional - only include links + files that exist. page (a dedicated /recordings/N landing page) takes priority for the primary 'Watch the recording' button; otherwise youtube renders a 'Watch on YouTube' button. share_topics[] feed the randomized 'I'm watching' Farcaster/X share buttons (one topic picked at random per click). Fields: date, title, presenter, handle, org, track, format, thumbnail, summary, topics[], recording, youtube, page, transcript, link, link_label, takeaways[], share_topics[].

recaps.title: Starting and growing your own livestream
recaps.summary: Day 2 and the first creator-track session of the season. Ohnahji shared his journey in live streaming - how he started, how he found his people, and how you grow an audience while you are still building everything else.
takeaways: How to start streaming and grow an audience while you build everything else.
Consistency beats production value early - the audience comes from showing up.
The first creator-track session of ZABAL Gamez Season 1.

recaps.title: Bonfire and the ZABAL Bonfire bot
recaps.summary: Day 1, builder track. Joshua.eth walked through Bonfire and the ZABAL Bonfire bot - the ecosystem's shared memory that bots and builds sit on top of - and how to point your own agent at it to follow ZABAL Gamez in real time. Plat0x went deeper on building on the graph.
takeaways: Point your agent at the ZABAL Bonfire to follow ZABAL Gamez in real time.
Bonfire is the ecosystem's shared memory - the layer bots and builds sit on top of.
Your build can read from and write to the graph, so it stays in sync with the season.

recaps.title: Empire Builder Part 2: the live build
share_topics: standing up a tokenless empire live
leaderboards scored by holders times boosters
adding a $Zabal token booster on the spot
reading boosters to find your real people
your main empire is the tree, sub-empires are the branches
the token is the amplifier, not the foundation
recaps.summary: Part 2 of the first ZABAL Gamez workshop - the live build that follows the Part 1 framework talk. yerbearserker (Jordan Oram) of Empire Builder gives a hands-on tour of the Empire tool stack from inside Farcaster, walks the top empires and how leaderboards score holders times boosters, runs a live Warplet raffle airdrop to DotA NFT holders, adds a $Zabal token booster, and stands up a fresh tokenless ZABAL Gamez empire on the spot, then wires two leaderboards (channel activity in /zabal and cast reactions).
takeaways: See a tokenless empire stood up live, start to finish.
Leaderboards score a base component multiplied by boosters - NFT holdings, token thresholds, or staking.
Distribution in practice: a live Warplet raffle airdrop to the most active DotA NFT holders.
A $Zabal token booster added live (10M minimum, 3x), funded from the treasury.

recaps.title: Empire Builder V3
share_topics: why you shouldn't launch a token yet
the Triple A framework: Assemble, Affirm, Ascend
the token is the amplifier, not the foundation
tactical generosity and rewarding your real people
generative not extractive, symbiotic not parasitic
reaching real holders, not Web2 cohorts
recaps.summary: Day 1 opener. yerbearserker (Jordan Oram), with diviflyy, co-founder of Empire Builder, walked through Empire Builder V3 - leaderboards, Empires, boosters, staking, and token mechanics - and stood up a tokenless ZABAL Gamez Empire live during the session. His message: build the foundation before you launch anything.
takeaways: Do not launch a token until you are ready - build the foundation first.
The Triple-A framework: Assemble, Affirm, Ascend.
Leaderboards, boosters, staking, and tactical generosity power a tokenless Empire.
yerbearserker stood up a tokenless ZABAL Gamez Empire live during the session.
